letter:
Safiyya,

Eid Mubarak.

Thirty days of fasting. Thirty mornings of quiet before the world woke up. Thirty nights of prayer that only you and Allah know the depth of.

And now — today — it ends and begins at once. The way all meaningful things do.

You have always carried your faith like something precious but not fragile. Not something to be hidden away, but something that walks with you into every room you enter.

This Eid, may you feel how seen you are. Not just by us — but by a Creator who has known you by name since before the world began.

Taqabbal Allahu minna wa minkum.
May Allah accept it from all of us.

messagesFromGroup.name: Baba
messagesFromGroup.message: Eid Mubarak ya habibti. Every year I watch you grow in grace and faith. My heart overflows with pride and with love.

messagesFromGroup.name: Mama
messagesFromGroup.message: From the little girl who used to wake me for suhoor to the woman you are now — you are my greatest blessing. Eid Mubarak, my love.

messagesFromGroup.name: Zainab
messagesFromGroup.message: We broke so many fasts together and prayed through so many nights. Alhamdulillah for you, my sister. Always.

messagesFromGroup.name: Aunt Fatou
messagesFromGroup.message: May this Eid bring you everything your heart has been quiet about. The things only dua holds. Eid Mubarak.

messagesFromGroup.name: Yusuf
messagesFromGroup.message: You make this family warmer simply by being in it. Eid Mubarak, Safiyya. Always so proud of you.

questions.correctAnswer: The sighting of the new moon
options: The first prayer of the day
The sighting of the new moon

questions.text: What is the charity given at the end of Ramadan called?

rewardItems: A handmade gift, chosen with care
An Eid dinner, just the family
A keepsake to remember this Eid
customClosingMessage: Eid Mubarak, Safiyya. May your days be blessed.
